summary.query: Assess BRAF as a therapeutic target in melanoma

briefing.context: BRAF is mutated in ~50% of cutaneous melanomas, overwhelmingly V600E (~90% of BRAF-mutant cases) and V600K (~5–10%). The MAPK pathway is constitutively activated, driving proliferation and survival. First-generation BRAF monotherapy (vemurafenib) showed dramatic initial responses but near-universal acquired resistance within 6–12 months. Second-generation doublet BRAF+MEK inhibition (dabrafenib+trametinib, encorafenib+binimetinib) became the standard of care for BRAF-mutant advanced melanoma, improving median PFS to ~12–14 months and 5-year OS to ~35%. The current frontier is triplet combinations (BRAF+MEK+ERK or +SHP2) to suppress adaptive reactivation, and integration with immune checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1 ± anti-CTLA-4) — with the COMBI-i/TRILOGY trials establishing feasibility but also overlapping toxicity. Rare non-V600 mutations (Class II/III: K601E, fusions, splice variants) remain harder to target and are under-represented in approved indications.

data_availability.note: Deep somatic mutation, CNV, RNA-seq, methylation data for 470 cutaneous melanomas; strong BRAF coverage; acral/mucosal subtypes under-represented
Multiple melanoma tumor microenvironment scRNA-seq datasets available; good coverage of immune infiltrates and phenotypic plasticity states relevant to BRAFi resistance
BRAF V600E is a somatic driver — germline GWAS has limited direct utility; pigmentation/nevi loci exist but not actionable for target assessment
Extensive BRAFi compound activity data; Class I/II selectivity profiling available; useful for next-generation inhibitor positioning
Dozens of active trials; triplet combinations and BRAFi+ICI combos actively enrolling as of 2024–2025
Strong BRAF-mutant melanoma cell line representation; functional essentiality and drug sensitivity data available; note 2D culture may not capture TME-driven resistance
Well-characterized resistance mechanisms: NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, BRAF amplification, splice variants, RTK bypass (EGFR, MET, IGF1R), phenotypic switch — robust prior art for hypotheses

priority_questions: What acquired resistance mechanisms dominate in patients who relapse on doublet BRAF+MEK therapy, and which are most pharmacologically tractable (e.g., ERK reactivation vs. lineage plasticity)?
Does upfront triplet therapy (BRAF+MEK+ERK inhibitor) delay resistance onset without prohibitive toxicity, and what biomarkers predict which patients benefit most?
What is the optimal sequencing or combination strategy with PD-1/CTLA-4 checkpoint blockade — concurrent vs. sequential, and do BRAFi-induced immune remodeling windows matter?
Are Class II/III non-V600 BRAF mutations addressable by next-generation type II inhibitors or degraders (e.g., PLX8394, LOXO-783), and how large is this unmet-need population?
Can transcriptomic or single-cell signatures of the invasive/mesenchymal phenotypic switch predict de novo resistance and stratify patients before treatment initiation?
feasibility_flags: Resistance mechanism heterogeneity is high — most datasets capture bulk tumor at a single timepoint; longitudinal biopsies are sparse, limiting conclusions about clonal evolution under treatment
BRAFi+ICI combination trials show overlapping immune-mediated toxicity (hepatitis, colitis) that may confound efficacy signal interpretation in retrospective analyses
Non-V600 BRAF mutation cohorts are small (<5% of cases each); any subgroup analysis will be underpowered without dedicated rare-mutation registries
Phenotypic plasticity (MITF-high vs. AXL/EGFR-high state switching) is context-dependent and poorly modeled in standard cell lines — DepMap data may underestimate this axis
Acral and mucosal melanoma subtypes have lower BRAF mutation rates and distinct biology; any analysis must stratify by subtype or risk conflating distinct disease entities

plan.question: What is the somatic mutation frequency of BRAF V600E/V600K vs. non-V600 (Class II/III) variants across TCGA SKCM, and how does it stratify by histological subtype (cutaneous vs. acral vs. mucosal)?
Is BRAF functionally essential in BRAF-mutant melanoma cell lines (DepMap CRISPR scores), and does essentiality correlate with V600 status vs. non-V600 class?

In single-cell melanoma atlases, what fraction of malignant cells (vs. stromal, endothelial, immune) express BRAF, and does expression differ between MITF-high (proliferative) and AXL/EGFR-high (mesenchymal/invasive) phenotypic states?
What is the normal-tissue expression profile of BRAF across human organs (GTEx/HPA), and are there high-expression tissues not covered by current clinical safety monitoring?
What post-market adverse event signals are recorded in FAERS for approved BRAFi agents (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) beyond the labelled cutaneous and hepatic events?

What do completed and ongoing clinical trials reveal about efficacy outcomes (ORR, PFS, OS) for BRAF+MEK doublet vs. triplet (BRAF+MEK+ERK/SHP2) vs. BRAFi+ICI combinations, and what biomarkers of response or resistance have been prospectively validated?

What is the trial success probability for BRAF-targeted therapies in melanoma given historical phase transition rates, and how does it compare across combination modalities (doublet, triplet, BRAFi+ICI)?

What is the current competitive and intellectual-property landscape for BRAF inhibitors, degraders (PROTACs), and allosteric inhibitors in melanoma — which mechanistic niches are crowded vs. whitespace?

division_findings.interpretation: OpenTargets returns 8 evidence items linking BRAF to melanoma [step_01], confirming a documented target–disease association. The somatic mutation frequency query did not return variant-level breakdown (V600E/V600K vs. Class II/III) or histological subtype stratification — the tool returned only aggregate evidence count, not TCGA mutation frequencies. Functional dependency via DepMap CRISPR scores was not executed; step_02 returned a deferred/tool-discovery status with no essentiality data [step_02]. The target rationale for BRAF in melanoma therefore rests on the OpenTargets association signal and prior clinical knowledge implicit in that aggregation, but neither variant-resolved somatic frequency nor functional essentiality data were produced in this run.
caveats: step_01 returned only an evidence count (n=8), not variant-level mutation frequencies — V600E/V600K vs. non-V600 stratification is absent
Histological subtype breakdown (cutaneous vs. acral vs. mucosal) was not returned
step_02 (DepMap CRISPR essentiality) was fully deferred — no functional dependency data available for this assessment
Without V600 status correlation, the finding cannot distinguish between the high-essentiality V600-mutant context and the more complex non-V600 (RAS-independent vs. RAS-dependent) contexts
OpenTargets evidence count alone does not confirm mechanism, causality, or therapeutic vulnerability

division_findings.interpretation: The single-cell localization query for BRAF expression in malignant vs. stromal/immune/endothelial compartments, and its distribution across MITF-high (proliferative) vs. AXL/EGFR-high (mesenchymal/invasive) phenotypic states, was not executed — the skill returned a deferred/tool-descriptor status requiring a ToolUniverse executor [step_03]. No quantitative cell-fraction or phenotypic-state data is therefore available from this run. What is known from the established literature (not from live tool output) is that BRAF V600E is a somatic driver mutation present in ~50% of cutaneous melanomas, and its expression is intrinsic to the malignant clone rather than a stromal signal — but this background cannot substitute for the missing single-cell atlas evidence requested. The right-tissue question — whether BRAF signal is malignant-cell-restricted and whether it varies by phenotypic state — remains unanswered by this run.
caveats: step_03 was deferred: no live single-cell tool output was returned; all tissue-compartment and phenotypic-state claims would rest on background knowledge, not evidence from this pipeline run.
Without malignant-cell fraction data, we cannot confirm that BRAF expression is tumor-cell-restricted rather than partly stromal or immune in the atlases queried.
Phenotypic-state heterogeneity (MITF-high vs. AXL/EGFR-high) is directly relevant to predicting intrinsic resistance to BRAF inhibitors; absence of this data is a material gap.
Background literature on BRAF V600E prevalence is not a substitute for single-cell localization evidence and must not be treated as confirming tissue specificity.

division_findings.interpretation: The CellMarker query for BRAF returned only 3 records — 2 cancer cell entries and 1 normal cell entry — providing minimal resolution on normal-tissue expression breadth [step_04]. This thin CellMarker result does not rule out safety-relevant off-target expression; it reflects a database coverage gap rather than genuine tissue restriction. The OpenFDA query for BRAF as a drug name returned an empty summary, likely because FAERS indexes by drug product name (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) rather than target gene symbol [step_05]. As a result, the post-market adverse event landscape cannot be characterised from this run. From established clinical knowledge, BRAFi class effects include paradoxical MAPK activation in RAS-wild-type tissues, cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, uveitis, pyrexia, and hepatotoxicity — but these are not confirmed by live skill output in this run.
caveats: CellMarker_search_by_gene with gene_symbol='BRAF' returned only 3 records; this reflects incomplete database indexing, not true tissue restriction — GTEx/HPA data were not queried [step_04].

OpenFDA query used 'BRAF' as drug_name rather than approved product names (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib), yielding an empty result; real FAERS signals for the BRAFi class are uncharacterised in this run [step_05].
No live GTEx or Human Protein Atlas data were returned, so high-expression normal tissues (brain, colon, thyroid) that may inform cardiac or neurological monitoring gaps remain unassessed.
Paradoxical ERK activation in RAS-wild-type cells — the dominant mechanistic safety liability of BRAFi — was not surfaced by either tool call and must be considered an unaddressed gap.
Evidence grade reflects skill execution gaps, not absence of real safety signals; the BRAFi class has a well-characterised clinical safety profile in the literature that this run did not capture.

division_findings.interpretation: The clinical trial search returned 67 trials for BRAF-targeted interventions in melanoma [step_06], confirming a mature and active development landscape. However, the step_06 tool call retrieved only a count summary without efficacy outcomes (ORR, PFS, OS) or prospectively validated biomarkers of response/resistance — the structured detail needed to differentiate doublet vs. triplet vs. BRAFi+ICI combinations is absent from the returned data. Step_07 was misrouted: the CellMarker cell-type marker lookup was executed instead of a trial success probability estimator, returning 3 records (2 cancer, 1 normal cell type) that are not relevant to phase transition rates or combination modality comparisons [step_07]. The breadth of 67 trials is consistent with BRAF V600E/K being a well-validated oncogenic driver in melanoma with established patient selection via companion diagnostics, but the division cannot derive confidence-level claims about comparative efficacy or resistance biomarkers from this run's raw output alone.
caveats: step_06 returned only a trial count (n=67), not trial-level efficacy data — ORR, PFS, OS, and biomarker endpoints are absent from the output.
step_07 was misrouted to a cell-marker tool (CellMarker_search_by_gene) rather than a trial success probability estimator; its output is irrelevant to right_patient assessment.
Without prospectively validated resistance biomarkers (e.g. NRAS, MEK1/2 mutations, BRAF amplification) from the tool output, resistance mechanism characterisation cannot be grounded here.
The 67-trial count likely spans all phases, indications within melanoma subtypes, and combination regimens — no stratification is available from the raw data.
Established clinical knowledge (vemurafenib, dabrafenib+trametinib approvals) is not substituted here per no-fabrication rules; only the tool output is interpreted.

division_findings.interpretation: The TheraSAbDab antibody database query for BRAF returned zero hits [step_08], indicating no approved or late-stage therapeutic antibodies targeting BRAF — consistent with BRAF being a predominantly intracellular kinase where small molecules dominate. This absence of antibody entries does not reflect the true competitive density of the BRAF space, which is heavily occupied by approved small-molecule inhibitors (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) and MEK combinations. The therapeutic antibody niche for BRAF is therefore genuinely open, but the broader small-molecule inhibitor landscape is mature and crowded. Novel mechanistic approaches — PROTACs, allosteric inhibitors, RAS-RAF interface disruptors — represent potential whitespace, but this tool query cannot confirm or size those opportunities.
caveats: TheraSAbDab covers only antibody-based therapeutics; the dominant BRAF modality (small molecules) is entirely outside this database's scope, making the zero-hit result uninformative about actual competitive crowding
No patent landscape, PROTAC pipeline, or small-molecule competitive analysis was returned — the commercial landscape assessment is therefore severely underpowered
The query result reflects a database boundary artifact, not a genuine signal of low competitive interest
Allosteric and degrader whitespace assessment would require separate IP and clinical-pipeline databases (e.g., ClinicalTrials, Cortellis, Integrity) that were not queried

synthesis.recommendation: BRAF is a clinically validated oncogenic driver in cutaneous melanoma with 8 OpenTargets evidence items [step_01] and 67 active clinical trials [step_06], and approved doublet BRAFi+MEKi therapy establishes proof-of-concept for the target class. However, this pipeline run produced severely underpowered evidence across all five 5R axes — functional essentiality (step_02 deferred), single-cell malignant-cell localization (step_03, step_06_reroute, step_07_reroute all failed), normal-tissue safety profile (step_04 CellMarker returned only 3 records), post-market AE signals (step_05 query error), and competitive landscape (step_08 wrong tool) — meaning the CONDITIONAL_GO rests entirely on external clinical knowledge implicit in the briefing, not on live pipeline evidence. Before any programme investment decision, the six experiments enumerated in proposed_experiments must be executed to ground the verdict in primary data.
synthesis.target_overview: BRAF is a serine/threonine kinase in the MAPK/ERK signalling cascade. Activating somatic mutations — predominantly V600E (~90% of BRAF-mutant cases) and V600K (~5–10%) — occur in approximately 50% of cutaneous melanomas, constitutively driving proliferation and survival independent of upstream RAS activation. Three ATP-competitive V600-selective inhibitors (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) are approved, used in doublet combinations with MEK inhibitors as standard of care for BRAF-mutant advanced melanoma. The therapeutic frontier encompasses triplet ERK/SHP2 combinations to suppress adaptive resistance reactivation, BRAFi+ICI combinations, and next-generation modalities (type II dimer-selective inhibitors, PROTACs, allosteric scaffolds) targeting non-V600 Class II/III mutations that remain clinically underserved.

liabilities.risk: Acquired resistance is near-universal within 6–12 months on doublet BRAFi+MEKi, driven by heterogeneous mechanisms (NRAS/MEK1/2 mutation, BRAF amplification/splice variants, RTK bypass via EGFR/MET/IGF1R, phenotypic mesenchymal switch) — no pipeline data was produced to quantify relative prevalence or pharmacological tractability of each mechanism [step_02 deferred, step_03 failed].
liabilities.mitigation: Prioritise longitudinal paired biopsy datasets (pre-treatment vs. progression) for clonal evolution profiling; run DepMap CRISPR essentiality stratified by resistance genotype to identify co-dependencies; evaluate triplet BRAFi+MEKi+ERKi or +SHP2i as upfront resistance prevention.

liabilities.risk: Single-cell malignant-cell localisation of BRAF expression was not obtained across three successive pipeline attempts [step_03, step_06_reroute, step_07_reroute all returned 0 records or deferred] — if BRAF is substantially expressed in stromal, endothelial, or immune compartments rather than restricted to the malignant clone, the therapeutic window narrows and normal-tissue toxicity risk rises beyond the on-tumour benefit.
liabilities.mitigation: Execute scrna-orchestrator query against Tirosh 2016/Jerby-Arnon 2018/TISCH2 melanoma atlases; compute BRAF log-normalised expression per annotated cell type (malignant vs. CAF vs. endothelial vs. myeloid); note that V600E mutation — not expression level — is the mechanistic selectivity determinant, so expression ubiquity alone does not preclude tumour-cell selectivity.

liabilities.risk: Normal-tissue BRAF expression safety profile is unmapped — step_04 returned only 3 CellMarker records (2 cancer, 1 normal), providing no organ-level quantification; GTEx/HPA data across 50+ tissues were never queried, leaving high-expression tissues (brain, colon, thyroid) and their associated on-target toxicity risks (paradoxical MAPK activation in RAS-WT cells, colorectal SCC induction, CNS effects) uncharacterised for next-generation agents.
liabilities.mitigation: Query GTEx v10 median TPM and HPA protein intensity for BRAF across all annotated tissues; cross-reference any tissue with TPM >10 against current BRAFi label safety monitoring requirements and known paradoxical-activation vulnerability of RAS-WT contexts.

liabilities.risk: Post-market adverse event profile for approved BRAFi agents (vemurafenib, dabrafenib, encorafenib) was not recovered — step_05 queried OpenFDA with drug_name='BRAF' (a gene symbol, not a drug name), returning an empty summary; unlabelled AEs (uveitis, QTc prolongation, secondary malignancies, cardiotoxicity) that may inform combination scheduling with ICI remain uncharacterised from this run.
liabilities.mitigation: Re-run OpenFDA_search_drug_events with drug_name set individually to 'vemurafenib', 'dabrafenib', and 'encorafenib'; count by MedDRA PT; apply PRR disproportionality to flag signals not on current labels.

liabilities.risk: Competitive and IP landscape is entirely uncharacterised — step_08 queried TheraSAbDab (an antibody database) for a small-molecule kinase target, returning 0 records; the small-molecule inhibitor, PROTAC, and allosteric inhibitor landscape was never assessed, leaving programme differentiation strategy undefined.
liabilities.mitigation: Run lit-synthesizer (Tavily+PubMed) sweep for BRAF PROTAC/degrader, allosteric/dimer-selective, pan-RAF, and brain-penetrant BRAFi programs from 2022–2025; map to company, clinical stage, and IP assignee to identify mechanistic whitespace.

liabilities.risk: Non-V600 BRAF mutations (Class II kinase-active dimers, Class III RAS-dependent; ~10–15% of BRAF-mutant melanoma) are not addressable by approved V600-selective inhibitors; structural and biochemical tractability data for type II inhibitors (PLX8394, LOXO-783) and PROTAC degraders in these contexts was not retrieved, leaving this unmet-need population undefined.
liabilities.mitigation: Query ChEMBL/BindingDB for Class II-selective binding data; run tractability profiler for non-V600 BRAF crystal structures; estimate addressable patient population from TCGA SKCM allele-frequency data stratified by histological subtype.

liabilities.risk: Phenotypic mesenchymal/AXL-high state switching — a resistance axis that is neither purely genetic nor pharmacologically addressed — was not profiled; no single-cell atlas data was recovered to define its prevalence or co-dependencies (AXL, EGFR, WNT5A), precluding patient stratification for combination or switch strategies.
liabilities.mitigation: Analyse MITF-high vs. AXL/EGFR-high state fractions in published scRNA melanoma atlases; test whether transcriptomic phenotypic-switch signature predicts de novo BRAFi resistance in TCGA SKCM bulk RNA data using GSVA or ssGSEA.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Re-query TCGA SKCM MAF via cBioPortal or GDC API for BRAF codon 600 (V600E, V600K) vs. non-V600 kinase-domain variants, stratified by annotated histological subtype (cutaneous, acral, mucosal); compute allele frequencies and 95% CIs per subtype.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Allele frequency table per subtype; V600E dominance expected in cutaneous (~90% of BRAF-mutant), lower in acral/mucosal — quantifies addressable patient population per modality.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_01 returned only an OpenTargets evidence count (n=8); without allele-level frequencies stratified by subtype, the genetic rationale for V600-selective vs. pan-BRAF vs. Class II-selective therapy cannot be differentiated, and the addressable population is undefined.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Pull DepMap 23Q4 CRISPR Chronos scores for BRAF across all SKCM cell lines via crispr-screen-triage; annotate by BRAF mutation status (V600E, V600K, non-V600 Class II/III, WT); compare mean Chronos scores across classes with Welch t-test and Mann-Whitney U.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Class I (V600E/K) lines expected Chronos < −0.5 (essential); Class III (RAS-dependent) lines may show attenuated or paradoxically positive scores; validates oncogene addiction in V600 context and informs whether non-V600 inhibition has efficacy return.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_02 was fully deferred; functional essentiality is the mechanistic anchor for 'right_target' — without it, the entire target hypothesis rests only on association counts from OpenTargets.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Query published single-cell melanoma atlases (Tirosh 2016 Science, Jerby-Arnon 2018 Cell, TISCH2 database) via scrna-orchestrator for BRAF log-normalised expression per annotated cell type (malignant SOX10+/MITF+, CAF FAP+/PDGFRA+, endothelial PECAM1+, T-cell CD3E+, myeloid CD68+); stratify malignant cells by MITF-high vs. AXL/EGFR-high phenotypic state.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: BRAF broadly expressed across compartments (ubiquitous RAF kinase) but functional V600E mutation restricted to malignant clone — confirms on-tumour selectivity is mutation-driven not expression-driven; MITF-high vs. AXL-high differential informs intrinsic resistance prediction.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Three consecutive pipeline attempts (step_03, step_06_reroute, step_07_reroute) failed to obtain this data; it is the single highest-priority gap before a therapeutic-window or safety verdict can be rendered.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Query GTEx v10 bulk RNA-seq median TPM and Human Protein Atlas protein intensity (IHC score) for BRAF across 50+ annotated tissues via celltype-specificity-profiler; flag any tissue with TPM >10 or HPA medium/high staining not explicitly covered by current BRAFi label safety monitoring.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Ranked organ expression table; high BRAF expression expected in brain and colon — cross-reference against known paradoxical MAPK activation risk in RAS-WT colorectal tissue and CNS penetration data for approved BRAFi agents.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_04 used CellMarker (cell-type marker DB) returning only 3 records — insufficient for a safety profile; GTEx/HPA are the authoritative quantitative sources for normal-tissue on-target toxicity risk assessment.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Re-run OpenFDA_search_drug_events with drug_name set individually to 'vemurafenib', 'dabrafenib', and 'encorafenib'; count MedDRA preferred terms; apply PRR/ROR disproportionality analysis to identify any PT with PRR >2 and N >3 not on the current approved label.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Top-20 disproportionate AE signals per drug; expected labelled signals (cutaneous SCC, pyrexia, hepatotoxicity); potential unlabelled signals (uveitis, QTc prolongation, second primary malignancy, cardiac events) to be flagged for ICI combination scheduling.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_05 used gene symbol 'BRAF' as drug_name, returning an empty summary that is an execution error not a true null signal; FAERS pharmacovigilance data for these approved drugs exists and is required for the right_safety verdict.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Extract ORR, median PFS, and OS from COMBI-d, COMBI-v, COLUMBUS, ANCHOR-CRC (control arm), and TRILOGY published Phase II/III trial readouts via lit-synthesizer PubMed sweep; compute phase II→III historical success rates for BRAFi doublet vs. triplet vs. BRAFi+ICI modalities.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Doublet (BRAF+MEK) ORR ~65–70%, median PFS ~12–14 months, 5-year OS ~35%; triplet and BRAFi+ICI benchmarks; phase transition success rates by modality — anchors any commercial or clinical differentiation recommendation.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_06 retrieved a trial count (n=67) but no outcomes; step_07 was misrouted to CellMarker — the clinical efficacy evidence chain is entirely absent from this pipeline run.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Run lit-synthesizer (Tavily+PubMed) sweep with queries: 'BRAF PROTAC degrader melanoma 2023 2024 2025', 'allosteric BRAF inhibitor αC-helix', 'dimer-selective BRAF inhibitor PLX8394 LOXO-783 clinical', 'pan-RAF inhibitor melanoma'; map hits to company, modality, clinical phase, and patent assignee.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Modality × development stage matrix; crowded niches: ATP-competitive V600E inhibitors (approved); emerging competition: dimer-selective agents; whitespace candidates: allosteric non-ATP site, BRAF PROTAC in solid tumours, brain-penetrant BRAFi for CNS metastases.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_08 queried TheraSAbDab (antibody DB, structurally wrong for a kinase target) returning 0 records; the entire commercial differentiation analysis must be rebuilt via literature and patent search.

proposed_experiments.experiment: For non-V600 BRAF mutations (Class II: K601E, fusions, exon 11 splice variants; Class III: D594G/N), query ChEMBL and BindingDB for binding affinities of type II inhibitors (sorafenib, LY3009120, PLX8394, LOXO-783) vs. V600E BRAF; cross-reference with available crystal structures in PDB; estimate addressable patient population from TCGA SKCM non-V600 allele frequencies.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: IC50/Kd table for type II inhibitors across BRAF mutation classes; structural tractability flag (druggable pocket confirmed vs. predicted); estimated annual patient count in Class II/III non-V600 cutaneous melanoma.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Non-V600 mutations represent the primary unmet need and differentiation opportunity from approved therapy; tractability here determines whether a Class II-selective or PROTAC programme has commercial rationale beyond V600.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Test whether a transcriptomic mesenchymal/AXL-high phenotypic switch signature (derived from Verfaillie 2015 or Tirosh 2016 gene sets) predicts de novo or acquired BRAFi resistance in TCGA SKCM bulk RNA-seq using GSVA/ssGSEA; validate against published BRAFi-resistant cell line RNA-seq datasets; assess AXL, EGFR, and WNT5A as co-dependency targets in AXL-high state.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Hazard ratio for OS/PFS stratified by MITF-high vs. AXL-high ssGSEA score in TCGA BRAF-mutant melanoma; identification of one or more co-dependency targets with confirmed expression enrichment and pharmacological tractability in the AXL-high state.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Phenotypic plasticity is the least pharmacologically addressed resistance axis; a validated transcriptomic biomarker would define a patient stratum for upfront combination or switch strategy and differentiate a next-generation programme from approved doublet BRAFi+MEKi.

proposed_experiments.expected_readout: BRAF broadly expressed across compartments (ubiquitous RAF kinase) but functional V600E mutation restricted to malignant clone — confirms on-tumour selectivity is mutation-driven not expression-driven; MITF-high vs. AXL-high differential informs intrinsic resistance prediction.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Three consecutive pipeline attempts (step_03, step_06_reroute, step_07_reroute) failed to obtain this data; it is the single highest-priority gap before a therapeutic-window or safety verdict can be rendered.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Query GTEx v10 bulk RNA-seq median TPM and Human Protein Atlas protein intensity (IHC score) for BRAF across 50+ annotated tissues via celltype-specificity-profiler; flag any tissue with TPM >10 or HPA medium/high staining not explicitly covered by current BRAFi label safety monitoring.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Ranked organ expression table; high BRAF expression expected in brain and colon — cross-reference against known paradoxical MAPK activation risk in RAS-WT colorectal tissue and CNS penetration data for approved BRAFi agents.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_04 used CellMarker (cell-type marker DB) returning only 3 records — insufficient for a safety profile; GTEx/HPA are the authoritative quantitative sources for normal-tissue on-target toxicity risk assessment.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Re-run OpenFDA_search_drug_events with drug_name set individually to 'vemurafenib', 'dabrafenib', and 'encorafenib'; count MedDRA preferred terms; apply PRR/ROR disproportionality analysis to identify any PT with PRR >2 and N >3 not on the current approved label.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Top-20 disproportionate AE signals per drug; expected labelled signals (cutaneous SCC, pyrexia, hepatotoxicity); potential unlabelled signals (uveitis, QTc prolongation, second primary malignancy, cardiac events) to be flagged for ICI combination scheduling.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_05 used gene symbol 'BRAF' as drug_name, returning an empty summary that is an execution error not a true null signal; FAERS pharmacovigilance data for these approved drugs exists and is required for the right_safety verdict.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Extract ORR, median PFS, and OS from COMBI-d, COMBI-v, COLUMBUS, ANCHOR-CRC (control arm), and TRILOGY published Phase II/III trial readouts via lit-synthesizer PubMed sweep; compute phase II→III historical success rates for BRAFi doublet vs. triplet vs. BRAFi+ICI modalities.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Doublet (BRAF+MEK) ORR ~65–70%, median PFS ~12–14 months, 5-year OS ~35%; triplet and BRAFi+ICI benchmarks; phase transition success rates by modality — anchors any commercial or clinical differentiation recommendation.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_06 retrieved a trial count (n=67) but no outcomes; step_07 was misrouted to CellMarker — the clinical efficacy evidence chain is entirely absent from this pipeline run.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Run lit-synthesizer (Tavily+PubMed) sweep with queries: 'BRAF PROTAC degrader melanoma 2023 2024 2025', 'allosteric BRAF inhibitor αC-helix', 'dimer-selective BRAF inhibitor PLX8394 LOXO-783 clinical', 'pan-RAF inhibitor melanoma'; map hits to company, modality, clinical phase, and patent assignee.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Modality × development stage matrix; crowded niches: ATP-competitive V600E inhibitors (approved); emerging competition: dimer-selective agents; whitespace candidates: allosteric non-ATP site, BRAF PROTAC in solid tumours, brain-penetrant BRAFi for CNS metastases.
proposed_experiments.rationale: step_08 queried TheraSAbDab (antibody DB, structurally wrong for a kinase target) returning 0 records; the entire commercial differentiation analysis must be rebuilt via literature and patent search.

proposed_experiments.experiment: For non-V600 BRAF mutations (Class II: K601E, fusions, exon 11 splice variants; Class III: D594G/N), query ChEMBL and BindingDB for binding affinities of type II inhibitors (sorafenib, LY3009120, PLX8394, LOXO-783) vs. V600E BRAF; cross-reference with available crystal structures in PDB; estimate addressable patient population from TCGA SKCM non-V600 allele frequencies.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: IC50/Kd table for type II inhibitors across BRAF mutation classes; structural tractability flag (druggable pocket confirmed vs. predicted); estimated annual patient count in Class II/III non-V600 cutaneous melanoma.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Non-V600 mutations represent the primary unmet need and differentiation opportunity from approved therapy; tractability here determines whether a Class II-selective or PROTAC programme has commercial rationale beyond V600.

proposed_experiments.experiment: Test whether a transcriptomic mesenchymal/AXL-high phenotypic switch signature (derived from Verfaillie 2015 or Tirosh 2016 gene sets) predicts de novo or acquired BRAFi resistance in TCGA SKCM bulk RNA-seq using GSVA/ssGSEA; validate against published BRAFi-resistant cell line RNA-seq datasets; assess AXL, EGFR, and WNT5A as co-dependency targets in AXL-high state.
proposed_experiments.expected_readout: Hazard ratio for OS/PFS stratified by MITF-high vs. AXL-high ssGSEA score in TCGA BRAF-mutant melanoma; identification of one or more co-dependency targets with confirmed expression enrichment and pharmacological tractability in the AXL-high state.
proposed_experiments.rationale: Phenotypic plasticity is the least pharmacologically addressed resistance axis; a validated transcriptomic biomarker would define a patient stratum for upfront combination or switch strategy and differentiate a next-generation programme from approved doublet BRAFi+MEKi.
